note: Balancer V3 Router v2 user pre-sign surface triage. Current manifests cover direct single-token swaps and plain multicall wrappers; liquidity, donation, and Permit2 batch wrapper functions remain explicit deferred gaps until dynamic pool-token mapping and Permit2 side-effect modeling are wired.

0x0ca078ec.name: addLiquidityCustom
0x0ca078ec.reason: deferred user liquidity path: custom userData and dynamic pool-token amount arrays require a pool token resolver before faithful add_liquidity emission

0x5b343791.name: addLiquidityHook
0x5b343791.reason: Vault callback hook path; users call Router entry points, not hook functions directly

0x724dba33.name: addLiquidityProportional
0x724dba33.reason: deferred user liquidity path: maxAmountsIn requires dynamic pool-token amount zipping before faithful add_liquidity emission

0x72657d17.name: addLiquiditySingleTokenExactOut
0x72657d17.reason: deferred user liquidity path: pool token identity is dynamic and needs pool metadata resolver wiring before faithful add_liquidity emission

0xc08bc851.name: addLiquidityUnbalanced
0xc08bc851.reason: deferred user liquidity path: maxAmountsIn requires dynamic pool-token amount zipping before faithful add_liquidity emission

0xbf6ee3fd.name: donate
0xbf6ee3fd.reason: donation path has no current ActionBody model and must not be mislabeled as swap/liquidity

0x026b3d95.name: initialize

0xac9650d8.name: multicall
0xac9650d8.reason: wrapper: recursively decodes bytes[] child Router calls so bundled user actions keep concrete ActionBody shapes

0x19c6989f.name: permitBatchAndCall
0x19c6989f.reason: deferred wrapper: real mainnet samples commonly contain liquidity child calls and Permit2 batch allowance side effects; keep fail-closed until child liquidity mapping and Permit2 side-effect modeling are faithful
